document.docAuthor: Takano, Kohei Takenaka, Gao, Jian-Jun, Hu, Yao-Guang, Li, Nan-Nan, Yafuso, Masako, Suwito, Awit, Repin, Rimi, Pungga, Runi Anak Sylvester, Meleng, Paulus Ak, Kaliang, Clement Het, Chong, Lucy & Toda, Masanori J.
document.docTitle: Colocasiomyia kinabaluana Toda & Takano 2021, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Phylogeny, taxonomy and flower-breeding ecology of the Colocasiomyia cristata species group (Diptera: Drosophilidae), with descriptions of ten new species

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Phylogeny, taxonomy and flower-breeding ecology of the Colocasiomyia cristata species group (Diptera: Drosophilidae), with descriptions of ten new species

paragraph.emphasis:
6)
Colocasiomyia kinabaluana Toda & Takano
, sp. nov.

subSubSection.paragraph: Description ( ♂ and ♀ ). Head. Supracervical setae 6–9 per side, distally more or less curved, longer than inner occipital setae. Cibarial, medial sensilla approximately 3 per side; posterior sensillum 1 per side. Supralateral setae outside prementum 3–4 per side.
Thorax. Anterior dorsocentral setae just beside transverse suture.

paragraph.materialsCitation:
Holotype .
♂ ( BORN ), “ Kota Kinabalu , Sabah , Malaysia ,
3.i.1999
, ex
Alocasia macrorrhizos , M.J. Toda
”.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Paratypes .
Malaysia : 23♂ , 10♀ , same data as the holotype
;
6♂ , ditto except
12.viii.2004
, K. T . Takano leg. ( BORN , SEHU )
.
Philippines : 7♂ , 6♀ , Legaspi , S. Luzon , 13°08’52”N 123°44’04”E ,
30.xi.2012
, ex
A . macrorrhizos , P.J. Matthews, M. Medecilo & J.R. Castillo
leg
.;
10♂ , 10♀ , Gandara , Samar , 12°00'56"N 124°47'43"E ,
2.xii.2012
, ex
A . macrorrhizos , P.J. Matthews, M. Medecilo & J.R. Castillo
leg
.;
2♂ , Tanuan , N. Leyte , 11°07'03"N 125°00'55"E ,
3.xii.2012
, ex
A . macrorrhizos , P.J. Matthews, M. Medecilo & J.R. Castillo
leg
.;
10♂ , 10♀ , Sabang , Palawan , 10°11'24"N 118°54'00"E ,
5.ii.2013
, ex
A . macrorrhizos , P.J. Matthews, E.M. Agoo & D.A. Madulid
leg. ( MPMP , SEHU )
.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 21.
Colocasiomyia kinabaluana Toda & Takano
,
sp. nov.
(♂ and ♀ paratypes and puparium from Kota Kinabalu, Sabah, Malaysia). A, Male abdominal sternites (ventral view); B, female abdominal sternites (ventral view); C, periphallic organs (lateral view); D, phallic organs (lateral view); E, ditto (ventral view); F, female terminalia (lateral view); G, oviscapt (lateral view); H, anterior spiracles of puparium (dorsal view); I, puparium (ventral habitus; inset: caudal-most portion); J, mouth hook; K, cephalopharyngeal skeleton. Scale bars: 0.1 mm in A–G and K, 1.0 mm in I.

subSubSection.paragraph: Distribution. Borneo (Sabah), Philippines (Luzon, Samar , Leyte , Palawan ).
Etymology. Referring to the type locality.
